integrations_researched.name: Anthropic Claude API (Python SDK)

key_functions: client.messages.create(..., stream=True) -> iterable of SSE events

error_handling.auto_retries: 2 retries by default for connection errors, 429, and >=500

api_patterns.streaming_pattern:
async with client.messages.stream(max_tokens=1024, messages=[...], model='claude-opus-4-6') as stream:
    async for text in stream.text_stream:
        print(text)

gotchas: Default timeout is 10 minutes - may need adjustment for faster UX
Non-streaming requests with large max_tokens will throw ValueError if expected >10min
Auto-retries (2 by default) mean failures may take longer to surface
Must use stream=True or .stream() for long-running requests to avoid idle connection drops
model parameter is required - no default model

integrations_researched.name: OpenAI ChatGPT API (Python SDK)

key_functions: client.responses.create(model, input, instructions) -> Response (newer Responses API)
api_patterns.responses_api_pattern:
response = client.responses.create(
    model='gpt-4o',
    instructions='System prompt here',
    input='User message here'
)
print(response.output_text)
api_patterns.verified_against: OpenAI official PyPI page + developers.openai.com quickstart

infrastructure.rate_limits: Varies by plan tier and model

gotchas: SDK has both Chat Completions API (older, stable) and Responses API (newer) - use Chat Completions for compatibility
gpt-4o-mini recommended as starting point, upgrade to gpt-4o for quality
Create one client instance and reuse it - don't create per-request
Both sync and async clients powered by httpx
Portuguese output quality may vary - test prompts carefully for language consistency

integrations_researched.name: NotebookLM Integration (notebooklm-py)

verified_package.name: notebooklm-py
verified_package.disclaimer: UNOFFICIAL - Uses undocumented Google APIs that can change without notice. Not affiliated with Google.

api_patterns.initialization:
async with await NotebookLMClient.from_storage() as client:
    # use client APIs

validation_pattern.description: For theological validation, use chat.ask() to query against loaded Catholic sources
validation_pattern.code:
result = await client.chat.ask(notebook_id, 'Validate this content against Church teaching: ...')
print(result.answer)  # Answer with inline citations [1], [2]
for ref in result.references:
    print(f'Citation {ref.citation_number}: Source {ref.source_id}')
    print(f'Cited text: {ref.cited_text}')

response_structure.ChatReference.source_id: ID of the source being cited

authentication.description: Opens a browser window for Google SSO login. Stores auth tokens locally at ~/.notebooklm/
authentication.auto_refresh: Tokens refresh automatically on auth errors

gotchas: CRITICAL: Uses UNDOCUMENTED Google APIs - can break at any time without notice
NOT affiliated with Google - community-maintained project
Recommended only for prototypes, research, and personal projects
Browser-based authentication required - cannot use simple API key
Auth tokens expire and need periodic refresh (handled automatically but may fail)
Heavy usage may face Google rate limiting/throttling
Async-only API (asyncio) - all methods are coroutines
Requires Python 3.10+ (stricter than other dependencies)
Playwright + Chromium installation needed for browser auth (~200MB+)
No SLA, no guaranteed uptime, no official support

notebooklm_enterprise_api.description: Official Google Cloud NotebookLM Enterprise API (Preview/Pre-GA)
notebooklm_enterprise_api.verdict: Overkill for single-user personal project. Requires paid Google Workspace Enterprise.

fallback_strategy.description: If notebooklm-py breaks, implement validation using Claude/GPT with Catholic source documents
fallback_strategy.approach: Load Catholic documents (Catechism, Vatican II docs) as context and use AI to validate against them
fallback_strategy.trade_off: Less comprehensive than NotebookLM but more reliable and under our control

integrations_researched.name: FastAPI (Backend Web Framework)

imports: from fastapi import FastAPI, Depends, HTTPException

gotchas: FastAPI >= 0.130.0 dropped Python 3.9 support (Feb 2026) - requires Python 3.10+
Use 'fastapi[standard]' to get uvicorn and other standard dependencies
Native async support pairs well with async NotebookLM and AI SDK clients
CORS middleware MUST be configured for React frontend communication

integrations_researched.name: SQLModel (Database ORM)

imports: from sqlmodel import SQLModel, Field, Session, create_engine, select
key_functions: SQLModel - base class combining SQLAlchemy + Pydantic
api_patterns.async_sqlite_setup:
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine, async_sessionmaker

engine = create_async_engine('sqlite+aiosqlite:///./verbum.db', echo=True)
async_session = async_sessionmaker(engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False)
api_patterns.verified_against: PyPI sqlmodel page + sqlmodel.tiangolo.com + FastAPI SQL databases tutorial

gotchas: For async SQLite, must install aiosqlite separately: pip install aiosqlite
Use expire_on_commit=False for async sessions (SQLAlchemy docs recommendation)
SQLite is perfect for single-user app - no DB server needed
SQLModel combines Pydantic + SQLAlchemy into single model class (DRY)
Created by same author as FastAPI - designed to work together seamlessly

integrations_researched.name: icalendar (Calendar Export)

api_patterns.export_pattern:
cal = Calendar()
cal.add('prodid', '-//Verbum//verbum.app//')
cal.add('version', '2.0')
event = Event()
event.add('summary', 'Publish: Content Title')
event.add('dtstart', scheduled_date)
event.add('description', 'Channel: YouTube')
cal.add_component(event)
with open('schedule.ics', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(cal.to_ical())

infrastructure.output_format: .ics file (compatible with Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, Outlook)

gotchas: Output is .ics file format - user downloads and imports into their calendar app
No direct Google Calendar API integration (would require google-api-python-client + OAuth)
Sufficient for low-priority delivery reminders feature
Alternative: 'ics' package (pip install ics) - more pythonic API but less mature

unverified_claims.claim: NotebookLM can provide <2 second response times for validation queries
unverified_claims.reason: notebooklm-py uses undocumented Google APIs with no SLA. Response times depend on Google's internal infrastructure, network latency, notebook size, and current load. No performance benchmarks available in documentation.

unverified_claims.claim: NotebookLM third-party library will remain stable and functional
unverified_claims.reason: notebooklm-py explicitly warns it uses undocumented APIs that can change without notice. The library is community-maintained (MIT license) and is NOT affiliated with Google. Google can break the API at any time. This is the biggest technical risk in the project.

unverified_claims.claim: NotebookLM handles the entire Catholic knowledge base without custom RAG
unverified_claims.reason: NotebookLM requires sources to be manually uploaded/linked to notebooks. The Catholic corpus (Catechism, Vatican II docs, papal encyclicals, etc.) must be added as sources to a NotebookLM notebook. This is a one-time setup but requires curating and uploading the right documents. NotebookLM has source limits per notebook.

unverified_claims.claim: AI generation will complete in <5 seconds
unverified_claims.reason: Depends on model choice, prompt length, and max_tokens. Claude and GPT-4o typically respond in 2-10 seconds for moderate-length content. Short-form scripts should be under 5s, but long-form scripts (10-min video) may exceed this. Streaming can improve perceived performance.

unverified_claims.claim: Portuguese SEO optimization via AI is consistently high quality
unverified_claims.reason: Both Claude and GPT-4o support Portuguese, but quality of SEO-specific suggestions (keywords, meta descriptions) in Portuguese was not independently verified. May require careful prompt engineering.

recommendations.recommendation: Implement NotebookLM integration behind an abstraction layer (interface/protocol) so the validation provider can be swapped. Build a fallback validation strategy using Claude/GPT with loaded Catholic source documents. This protects against notebooklm-py breaking due to Google API changes.

notebooklm-py requires browser-based Google authentication (not a simple API key). The setup flow requires running 'notebooklm login' CLI command + Playwright/Chromium. For production stability, use NOTEBOOKLM_AUTH_JSON env var with pre-captured tokens and implement automatic token refresh handling.
Use Python 3.10+ as the minimum version. FastAPI >=0.130.0 and notebooklm-py both require Python 3.10+. The existing project uses Python 3.11+ which satisfies all dependencies.

Use SQLite with SQLModel + aiosqlite for async operations. SQLite is ideal for this single-user application - no database server needed, file-based storage, zero configuration. SQLModel provides Pydantic validation + SQLAlchemy ORM in a single model class, designed to integrate seamlessly with FastAPI.

Use async throughout the backend: AsyncAnthropic for Claude, AsyncOpenAI for ChatGPT, notebooklm-py (already async-only), FastAPI (native async), SQLModel with async sessions. This ensures the single-threaded event loop can handle concurrent AI and database operations efficiently.

Implement streaming responses for AI content generation using SSE (Server-Sent Events). Both Anthropic and OpenAI SDKs support streaming. FastAPI supports SSE via StreamingResponse. This dramatically improves perceived performance for the <5 second generation target.

The existing project already has React 18 + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS 3.x. The new Verbum feature should be built as additional routes/pages within the existing frontend, reusing the component library (Button, Input, Select, etc.) and Tailwind theme. No new frontend framework needed.

Use the Chat Completions API (client.chat.completions.create) rather than the newer Responses API for stability and wider documentation coverage. Start with gpt-4o-mini for cost efficiency, allow user to select gpt-4o for higher quality.

Use the icalendar library for simple .ics file export. This is low priority and sufficient for delivery reminders. Avoid Google Calendar API integration (requires OAuth complexity) unless specifically requested.

dependency_install_command: pip install anthropic openai "notebooklm-py[browser]" "fastapi[standard]" sqlmodel aiosqlite python-dotenv icalendar
